title: Phyllorkis pachyrachis
taxonomy: Phyllorkis pachyrachis is a species of orchid, and as such, it belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This family is part of the larger order Asparagales, which also includes other plants like asparagus and hyacinths. The order Asparagales is further grouped into the clade Monocots, which contains plants with one cotyledon in their seeds. Monocots are a subset of the angiosperms, the flowering plants. The plant kingdom is divided into several subgroups, with Angiosperms being one of the major groups.

intro: Phyllorkis pachyrachis, also known as the "Himalayan Oak Orchid", is a plant species belonging to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Phyllorkis pachyrachis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
